title: Velvety Japanese Cabbage Bowl
description: A citrusy, aerated, indulgent bowl featuring hepsi sweetheart cabbage in a fluffy egg custard, reminiscent of oyakodon.
instructions: In a bowl, beat the eggs with a pinch of salt.
Whisk soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar, ginger, and garlic in a small saucepan until the sugar dissolves.
Add water to the saucepan and bring to a gentle simmer, then pour the hot mixture over the beaten eggs, stirring gently until the eggs start to set.
In a separate pan, heat sesame oil over medium heat and add the sliced onion, cooking until translucent.
Add the cabbage to the pan, stir, and cook until it wilts slightly, about 3 minutes.
Pour the egg mixture over the cabbage and onions, cover, and let steam until the eggs fully set, about 2 minutes.
Transfer the cooked mixture to serving bowls, sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds, and drizzle with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice for a citrusy finish.
